skill.name: bolder
skill.description: Amplifies safe or underwhelming interface designs into more distinctive, memorable, and energetic experiences while preserving usability.
skill.summary: Make interface designs more visually confident without sacrificing accessibility or function.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 21 potential issues in SKILL.md, mostly weak cryptography and reconnaissance labels. Manual review found design guidance only, with no executable code, command execution, filesystem access, network access, secret handling, or prompt injection attempt. The static matches are false positives caused by ordinary design vocabulary.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Security Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance labels map to design prose, not security-sensitive behavior. Lines 3, 6, 11, 15, 21, 41, 52, 54, 59, 67, 102, 110, and 119 describe bold visual design. Lines 25, 66, 92, and 117 describe visual assessment, palette choices, composition, and quality review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains only natural-language design instructions and no executable code paths. The flagged terms are visually oriented words such as safe, bold, weakness, risk, and static.

content.user_title: Make Interfaces Bolder Without Losing Usability
content.value_statement: Many interfaces feel generic because every element has similar weight and energy. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward stronger visual hierarchy, personality, and motion while keeping the product usable.
actual_capabilities: Identifies why an interface feels too safe, generic, or visually flat.
Suggests stronger typography, color, spacing, composition, effects, and motion.
Encourages a clear focal point and a defined personality direction.
Warns against common AI-generated visual patterns that look generic.
Includes usability, accessibility, performance, and coherence checks.
limitations: It provides design direction, not a standalone visual asset library.
It depends on project context and may ask for brand or audience details.
It references a separate frontend-design skill for broader design rules.
It does not replace user testing or formal accessibility audits.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Bland Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn a plain marketing page into a stronger first impression with clearer hierarchy, richer visuals, and more memorable composition.

use_cases.title: Strengthen Product Screens
use_cases.description: Improve dashboards, settings pages, or tools that feel too uniform while preserving task clarity and scanability.

use_cases.title: Guide Creative Review
use_cases.description: Use a structured checklist to critique whether a redesign is distinctive, coherent, accessible, and performant.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Boldness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bolder to review this interface. Identify why it feels too safe and suggest practical changes that keep it usable.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Component Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bolder on the hero section. Propose stronger typography, color, spacing, and motion while preserving accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: Full Page Redesign Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bolder for this page. Choose one personality direction, define the focal point, and provide an implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Design Quality Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate a bold redesign before shipping.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bolder to audit this redesign. Check for generic AI patterns, visual coherence, accessibility, performance, and whether the bold choices support the product goal.

output_examples.input: Review a safe SaaS dashboard that uses small headings, gray cards, and equal spacing everywhere.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify flat hierarchy, timid scale, and low contrast. It would recommend one dominant focal area, stronger type scale, selective color emphasis, and denser supporting areas.

output_examples.input: Make a product launch page feel more memorable without using common gradient effects.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a distinct personality direction, larger hero typography, custom imagery or textures, asymmetric composition, and restrained motion tied to the message.

output_examples.input: Check whether a bold redesign went too far.
output_examples.output: The skill would evaluate readability, accessibility, motion load, coherence, and task completion. It would separate useful drama from distracting decoration.

best_practices: Provide the product goal, audience, brand constraints, and current interface before asking for changes.
Ask for one clear focal point so the design has contrast instead of constant intensity.
Verify bold choices against accessibility, performance, and task completion before shipping.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for more effects without explaining the product goal or audience.
Do not make every element visually loud, because hierarchy depends on contrast.
Do not accept trendy gradients, glass effects, or neon accents when they do not fit the brand.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents make interface designs more distinctive, energetic, and memorable while keeping them usable.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: It can guide code changes when used with a coding agent, but the skill itself is design guidance.

faq.question: Is it only for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can support landing pages, product screens, dashboards, components, and design reviews.

faq.question: How does it avoid generic AI design?
faq.answer: It explicitly warns against common patterns such as predictable gradients, glass effects, and decoration without purpose.

faq.question: Does it consider accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the agent to preserve readability, meet accessibility standards, and avoid overwhelming motion.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Share the current interface, brand personality, target audience, product purpose, and any accessibility or performance constraints.
